questions.question: Why is it risky to build your entire business on 'Rented Land' (Social Media) without owning a community?

options.text: The algorithm can change overnight and kill your reach, whereas you control the access to your own community.
You can't post videos on social media.

questions.explanation: Rented land (LinkedIn/Twitter) is for attention. Owned land (Email/Community) is for retention. A change in algorithm can wipe out your 'Top of Funnel,' but it can't touch your community.

questions.question: What is the primary difference between a SaaS Community and a Creator Community?

options.text: SaaS communities are for features/support; Creator communities are often the paid product itself (transformation).
SaaS communities are only for developers.

questions.explanation: In SaaS, the software is the value. In a Creator business, the *peer group and access to the creator* is often the value users pay for.

questions.question: Which platform is described as 'Facebook Groups meets Course Hosting' and is ideal for gamified learning?
questions.explanation: Skool is designed specifically for 'Community-Powered Learning' with built-in leaderboards and course hosting, making it the current favorite for creators.

questions.question: What is the 'Community Flywheel' described in this lesson?

options.text: Posting on Twitter until you go viral.
Public Content -> Free Bridge Community -> Paid 'Inner Circle' Community.
Cold emailing people to join your Discord.

questions.explanation: The flywheel moves people from 'Attention' (Public) to 'Nurture' (Free Community) to 'Monetization' (Paid Community), creating a sustainable machine.

questions.question: What is the '90-9-1' rule of community participation?

options.text: 90% lurk (read only), 9% contribute, 1% create (you/mods). This is normal.
90% of your members will leave in month 1.

questions.explanation: Founders often panic when 'everyone isn't posting.' But the vast majority of members (90%) are 'Lurkers' who get value just by reading. Do not optimize only for the noisy 10%.

questions.question: Audit the top 3 'Competitor Communities' in your space. Are they using Skool, Circle, or Discord? What is their 'Hook' (e.g., 'The #1 place to learn X')? Based on this, which platform 'Vibe' fits your specific audience best?
